titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Louis Mayer Rabinowitz Collection
titlestmt.author: Compiled by Janet Elaine Gertz

conventiondeclaration.abbr: finding aid created in accordance with manuscripts and archives' processing manual.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday October 26, 2018 at 08:09

did.unittitle: Louis Mayer Rabinowitz collection
did.abstract: Correspondence, volumes, and other papers collected by Rabinowitz. Includes autograph letters of Henry Blackwell (1851-1928), Charles Darwin (1809-1882), Samuel Huntington (1731-1796), and Roger Sherman (1721-1793).

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Louis Mayer Rabinowitz Collection (MS 1044). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This autograph collection compiled by Louis M. Rabinowitz contains several letters by British and European notables such as Charles Darwin, a number of letters and documents signed by well-known Americans of the eighteenth and nineteenth centuries, such as John C. Calhoun and Roger Sherman, and a Latin handbook of logic from the eigtheenth century, but the bulk of the collection is made up of papers of Henry Blackwell (1851-1928). Included are letters to Blackwell from a variety of individuals from Wales and elsewhere interested in Welsh history and literature, and two copies of Blackwell's bibliography of materials about Wales published in the United States, with a number of newspaper clippings found in the volumes. The letters were for the most part removed from books belonging to Blackwell which Rabinowitz donated to Yale University. The final item in the collection is a reminiscence of Rabinowitz by James T. Babb.

Additional printed and manuscript material has been given to the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library by Louis Rabinowitz. For a description of this material, the researcher should speak to the reference staff at the Beinecke Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Manufacturer and philanthropist; born in Rossane, Lithuania; came to the United States in 1901; founded L.M. Rabinowitz & Co. in 1917 in New York City, served as president from 1917-1957; organized the Lamar Slide Fastener Co. and served as president, 1939-1954; known for his philanthropic gifts; in 1944 he established the Louis M. Rabinowitz Foundation; foundation inaugurated a research and publication program in Hebrew lore and literature at Yale.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright status for collection materials is unknown, though much of the material in this collection is likely in the public domain. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The collection is open for research.

did.unittitle: Inventory
Autograph letters
Booker, Luke, 1762-1835: ALS to unidentified, removed from The Springs of Plynlimmon , 1834, by Rev. Luke Booker, belonging to Henry Blackwell
Calhoun, John Caldwell, 1782-1850: ALS to John A. Calhoun
Chapman, Mary Blackwell Sterling: ALS to Henry Blackwell, removed from Sir Thomas Malory, The Story of Sir Galahad…Retold by Mary Blackwell Sterling , c. [1908]
Columbia Publishing Company: TLS to Henry Blackwell, giving a brief history of Columbia , a weekly newspaper "published in the interest of the Welsh people and their descendants in the United States"
Daniels, R T., d.1896: ALS to Henry Blackwell, giving the history of Y Wasg , the second Welsh newspaper published in Pittsburg
Darwin, Charles, 1809-1882: ALS to unidentified
Davies, John: 3 ALS to Henry Blackwell, removed from The Celtic Element in the Dialects... , by John Davies
Evans, Samuel J.: ALS to Henry Blackwell, removed from Studies in Welsh Phonology , 1909
Furness, J R.: ALS to Henry Blackwell, removed from King Henry the VII , 1900
Hadley, Alice: 2 ALS to Henry Blackwell, concerning the Conway parish register, which Hadley was preparing
Haines, W.: ALS to Henry Blackwell, removed from An Account of the Presentation of the Colours… , by Charles Heath
Halliday, George Eley: 2 ALS to Henry Blackwell, removed from Llandaff Church Plat e, 1901
Hathaway, Matthew: signed receipt issued to Roger Sherman
Hendrickson, Rev. Wm. H.: ALS to Henry Blackwell
Horton, E M.: ALS to Henry Blackwell, concerning Rev. James Powell
Hughes, H. Seymour: 2 ALS to Henry Blackwell, removed from The Registers of Llantrithyd, Glamorganshire , 1888
Hughes, Joshua, bp. of St. Asaph, 1807-1889: ALS to Henry Blackwell, concerning Richard Robarts Jones ("Dick of Aberdaron")
Humphreys, Daniel: signed receipt to Roger Sherman
Humphreys, David, 1752-1818: ALS to James Monroe, with typed transcript
Huntington, Samuel
Commission to Theophilus Miles Smith as ensign of 5th Company, 2nd Regiment, Connecticut Militia
Copy of letter to General Knox
Commission to Jared Huntington as captain of 14th Regiment, Connecticut Militia
Johnes, Thomas, 1748-1816: ALS to Charles Taylor, removed from An Attempt to Describe Hafod… , by George Cumberland, 1796, belonging to Henry Blackwell
Jones, Charles Colcock, 1831-1893: ALS to Henry Blackwell, removed from Button Gwinnett , by Charles C. Jones, 1884
Leete, William, ca.1613-1683: LS to John Leverett, Governor of Massachusetts, and the Hon. Council with him at Boston
Levick, James Jones, 1824-1893: ALS to Henry Blackwell, removed from John Ap Thomas and His Friends , 1884
Lewis, Kinnersley: ALS to Henry Blackwell, removed from Hymn to the Eternal , 1886
Matthews, T: 5 ALS to Henry Blackwell, removed from Welsh Records in Paris , 1910
Morgan, Thomas: ALS to Henry Blackwell, removed from Handbook of the Origin of Place-Names in Wales and Monmouthshire , 1887
Morris, Edward Dafydd, 1825-1915: ALS to Henry Blackwell
Powell, Ella J. (Mrs. James): 2 ALS to Henry Balckwell concerning her husband
Pritchard, Emily M.: 2 ALS to unidentified
Sherman, Roger, 1721-1793: summons to Safford Stevens of Canaan, Connecticut, to appear before the County Court at New Haven
Thomas, D R.: ALS to unidentified
Thomas, Isaiah
ALS to Messrs. Hudson and Goodwin
ALS to Isaac Beers
Thomas, W. Glawthorne: ALS to Bishop of St. Asaph (Joshua Hughes), enclosed in ALS from Joshua Hughes to Henry Blackwell
Tyler, John, 1790-1862: ALS to Dr. Sprague
Vidocq, Eugène François, 1775-1857: ALS to M. le Prefet, removed from Vidocq's Mémoires , 1828
Williams, Sir John, 1840-: ALS to Henry Blackwell, removed from An Address Delivered… Oct. 27, 1904, by Sir John Williams
Williams, Richard
ALS to Henry Blackwell, with copy of Williams' title page to his Welsh translation of Bacon's Essays , removed from A History of the Parish of Llanbrynmair , 1889
2 ALS to Henry Blackwell, removed from Montgomeryshire Worthies , 1884
Volumes
Blackwell, Henry, 1851-1928: "Bibliography of What Has Been Published in the United States Relating to Wales and the Welsh from the Earliest to the Present Time"
Bound manuscript
Bound typescript
Logica , a handbook of logic in Latin
Other paper
Newspaper clippings in Welsh and English, removed from "Bibliography of What Has Been Published..."
"Louis Mayer Rabinowitz," by James T. Babb
